summary: Regression + new-features iteration. All 7 backend pytest cases pass. New UI features verified: (1) P badge is bright red (rgb(225,6,0)) and square (border-radius 0px, 26x26). (2) LockedRow rendered for 'Licence type', 'Permit status', and 'Proficiency' (no input element present). (3) Date picker Done closes the overlay and persists the draft (Save flow round-trips via PUT). (4) Cross-device sync via 4s polling works in both directions: a new account created from context B becomes loginnable in context A within ~6s with no manual refresh; an edit to addressLine1 done in context B propagates to context A's licence view within ~6s.

issues: react-native-web console warnings persist: deprecated 'shadow*' style props and props.pointerEvents. Non-blocking.

action_items: (Optional) Migrate deprecated react-native-web shadow*/pointerEvents to remove console warnings.
(Optional) Add a unique compound index on (digits, letters) in MongoDB to enforce uniqueness at the DB level and harden PUT against producing clashes.
(Optional) Split index.tsx (~2271 lines) into smaller modules per the >700 line guideline.
critical_code_review_comments: WheelColumn.onScroll now commits value live via onChange(clamped) -- good. Note that on RN-Web, onScroll fires for every frame; using scrollEventThrottle={16} keeps it reasonable but the parent setDraft on every frame will re-render the whole edit modal. Consider local state inside WheelDatePicker and only propagate to draft on snap/done for better perf on low-end devices.

Polling refresh every 4s issues a network round-trip even when the user is idle on the login screen. For a wallet app this is OK, but consider pausing the poll while screen==='login' to save bandwidth and battery, or use a longer interval there.

Cross-device edit sync uses naive setAccounts(data) on every poll, which can overwrite a draft the local user has typed but not saved if their edit modal is open while a remote edit lands. Edit-modal isolates `draft` state from `accounts` so it is safe in this app, but be careful if future code reads directly from accounts inside the edit modal.

Date picker Done handler does `await onUpdateLicence(draft)` -- meaning every date change writes to backend. If user opens picker then taps Done without scrolling, an unnecessary PUT still fires. Minor; consider diffing draft vs server value before PUT.
test_credentials: Admin digits=4095 letters=QUAYDE. Test accounts created during run (TEST_T2_User 777111/ZZZ, TEST_SyncTest 555444/AAA) were deleted at the end.
seed_data_creation: None left behind -- all TEST_* accounts cleaned via DELETE /api/accounts/{id}.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_accounts_api.py is the single source of regression tests (7 tests, all passing). Frontend testIDs available: admin-button, admin-digits, admin-letters, admin-submit, admin-create-open, admin-exit, create-name, create-digits, create-letters, create-save, login-digits, login-letters, login-submit, reveal-qr (opens edit modal), save-edit, date-Issue date, date-Expiry, date-Date of birth, date-done, date-cancel, logout-button. Cross-device polling interval is 4s; allow >=6s for sync verification. Note: outside-the-browser HTTP clients (curl/urllib) need a Mozilla User-Agent header to bypass the preview proxy's 403; browser fetch works fine. The dateCard scroll wheels are RN-Web ScrollView and cannot be driven by setting scrollTop on plain divs -- use mouse.wheel or rely on round-trip verification via /api/accounts to confirm date changes.
rca of the issue: N/A -- no bugs found in this iteration.
